title: silene cucubalus var. bastardii
intro: Silene cucubalus var. bastardii, also known as a variant of the Red Campion, is a flowering plant species within the Caryophyllaceae family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to exhibit various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, due to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Herbal preparations of Silene cucubalus var. bastardii can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function and combat infections.
